Tool_age_Description: Exact age in years, months, days between two dates — useful for forms and eligibility checks.
Tool_age_Subtitle: Years, months, and days between dates
Tool_age_LearnText: Enter a date of birth (or any start date) and an as-of date. The tool returns the calendar age in years, months, and days, plus total days lived — handy for exams, schemes, and paperwork.
Tool_age_FormulaNote: Uses calendar month lengths (not a fixed 30-day month). End date must be on or after the start date.
Tool_age_ToHint: Defaults to today; change to compare ages on a future date.
Tool_age_ScenarioSubtitle: Age today vs one and five years ahead
Tool_age_ExampleStep_3: Same month and day → months = 0, days = 0.
Tool_age_Step_1: Enter from date (DOB) and to date.
Tool_age_Step_2: Subtract years; borrow a year if months go negative.
Tool_age_Step_3: Subtract months; borrow days from the previous month if needed.
Tool_age_Step_4: Read years, months, days (and optional total days).
Tool_age_Info_Purpose: Compute exact age or span between two calendar dates.
Tool_age_Info_WhyKnow: Many forms and eligibility rules need precise age, not just year of birth.
Tool_age_Info_RealLife: Exam age limits, senior-citizen cut-offs, scheme eligibility.
Tool_age_Info_Goal_2: Compare age on a future date
Tool_age_Info_Goal_3: Know total days in the span
Tool_age_Info_Alt_Sip_When: When planning investments over years of age.
Tool_age_Info_Alt_Goal_When: For goals tied to a future age.
Tool_age_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows calendar Y/M/D plus total days in one simple view.
Tool_age_Info_Motto: Know your exact age — not just the year.
Tool_forex_Description: Convert foreign currency to INR at your rate — with optional bank or card fee %.
Tool_forex_LearnText: Enter the foreign amount and the INR you get per unit (bank TT, card, or cash rate). Optionally add a fee or markup percent to see what you actually receive after costs.
Tool_forex_FormulaNote: Rate is INR per 1 foreign unit. Fee is applied on the converted INR amount.
Tool_forex_FeeHint: Optional — bank spread, card forex markup, or cash premium.
Tool_forex_ScenarioSubtitle: Same amount and rate, different fee %
Tool_forex_ExampleStep_4: Compare with 0% fee to see the true cost of the channel.
Tool_forex_ExampleStep_5: For outbound remittance, also check TCS rules separately.
Tool_forex_Step_1: Enter foreign amount and INR per unit.
Tool_forex_Info_Purpose: Convert foreign currency to INR and estimate fee impact.
Tool_forex_Info_WhyKnow: Quoted mid-market rates hide bank and card markups that cut what you receive.
Tool_forex_Info_RealLife: Travel cards, remittances, and online purchases in USD/EUR.
Tool_forex_Info_Alt_Tcs_When: For LRS remittance tax collected at source.
Tool_forex_Info_Alt_Tax_When: For overall tax planning after forex income.
Tool_forex_Info_Alt_Fd_When: After converting, to park INR proceeds.
Tool_forex_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows converted INR, fee, and net side by side for any rate you enter.
Tool_forex_Info_Motto: Rate is not enough — check the fee.
Tool_monthly_budget_Description: Track income vs needs, wants, and savings — with a 50/30/20 suggestion.
Tool_monthly_budget_LearnText: Split monthly cash into needs (must-pay), wants (lifestyle), and savings. See surplus or deficit versus income, and compare your split to the classic 50/30/20 rule.
Tool_monthly_budget_FormulaNote: 50/30/20 is a starting guide — adjust for high rent cities or aggressive debt payoff.
Tool_monthly_budget_NeedsHint: Rent, groceries, EMIs, utilities, school fees.
Tool_monthly_budget_ScenarioSubtitle: Your numbers vs 50/30/20 vs a tighter save rate
Tool_monthly_budget_ExampleStep_5: Use surplus to boost savings or cut wants if you are in deficit.
Tool_monthly_budget_Step_2: Enter needs, wants, and savings amounts.
Tool_monthly_budget_Info_Purpose: See if your month balances and how it compares to 50/30/20.
Tool_monthly_budget_Info_WhyKnow: Without a simple split, lifestyle creep quietly kills savings.
Tool_monthly_budget_Info_RealLife: Salaried households setting a monthly money plan.
Tool_monthly_budget_Info_Alt_Emergency_W: To size a cash buffer from monthly expenses.
Tool_monthly_budget_Info_Alt_Goal_When: For long-term goals funded from savings.
Tool_monthly_budget_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra combines surplus tracking with a clear 50/30/20 comparison.
Tool_monthly_budget_Info_Motto: Spend with a plan — save on purpose.
Tool_net_worth_Description: Add assets, subtract liabilities — see your net worth in one place.
Tool_net_worth_LearnText: List cash, investments, property, and other assets; then loans and credit-card dues. Net worth is what you own minus what you owe — a snapshot of financial progress over time.
Tool_net_worth_FormulaNote: Use realistic market values for property and investments. This is a snapshot, not a cash-flow plan.
Tool_net_worth_ExampleStep_4: Debt-free scenario would show net = full assets.
Tool_net_worth_ExampleStep_5: Track this number yearly to see progress.
Tool_net_worth_Step_1: Sum cash, investments, property, other assets.
Tool_net_worth_Step_2: Sum home loan, other loans, cards, other dues.
Tool_net_worth_Info_Purpose: Measure what you own minus what you owe.
Tool_net_worth_Info_WhyKnow: Income alone hides leverage; net worth shows true progress.
Tool_net_worth_Info_RealLife: Annual wealth check for families with home loans and SIPs.
Tool_net_worth_Info_Alt_Goal_When: To grow net worth toward a target.
Tool_net_worth_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra gives a clean assets-vs-liabilities net worth with debt scenarios.
Tool_net_worth_Info_Motto: Own more than you owe — track it.
Tool_child_education_Description: Inflate today’s education cost to the future, then find the monthly SIP to fund it.
Tool_child_education_LearnText: Education costs rise faster than general inflation. Enter today’s fee estimate, years until needed, education inflation, and expected investment return — get future cost and the SIP via sipRequired.
Tool_child_education_FormulaNote: Education inflation in India is often assumed around 8–12%. Returns are illustrative, not guaranteed.
Tool_child_education_CostHint: Today’s total for the course / degree you have in mind.
Tool_child_education_RateHint: Expected annual return on the education SIP.
Tool_child_education_ScenarioSubtitle: Same future cost, different SIP returns
Tool_child_education_ExampleStep_4: Lower return needs a higher SIP; higher return lowers it.
Tool_child_education_ExampleStep_5: Review cost and inflation every few years as fees change.
Tool_child_education_Step_1: Enter today’s education cost and years left.
Tool_child_education_Info_Purpose: Estimate future education cost and the SIP to fund it.
Tool_child_education_Info_WhyKnow: Waiting until admission year makes fees feel sudden and unaffordable.
Tool_child_education_Info_RealLife: Parents planning for college, IIT/IIM, or study abroad.
Tool_child_education_Info_Alt_Sip_When: When you already know the target corpus.
Tool_child_education_Info_Alt_Goal_When: For any goal amount without inflation on cost.
Tool_child_education_Info_Alt_Edu_When: If you will borrow part of the fee.
Tool_child_education_Info_Differentiatio: FinYatra inflates education cost then applies sipRequired in one flow.
Tool_child_education_Info_Motto: Inflate the fee — then fund the SIP.
Tool_80g_donation_Description: Estimate Section 80G deduction and tax saving — 50% or 100%, with or without 10% income limit.
Tool_80g_donation_Subtitle: Deduction and tax saving at your slab
Tool_80g_donation_LearnText: Section 80G (old regime) lets you claim 50% or 100% of eligible donations. Some donations are capped at 10% of adjusted income. Enter amount, eligibility, limit flag, income, and slab to see deduction and approximate tax saving.
Tool_80g_donation_FormulaNote: Illustrative only — check the trust’s 80G category and keep receipts. New regime generally does not allow 80G.
Tool_80g_donation_EligHint: Depends on the approved fund / trust category.
Tool_80g_donation_LimitHint: Some 80G donations have a 10% of AGI qualifying limit.
Tool_80g_donation_ExampleStep_1: 10% of income = 1,20,000; donation 50,000 is within limit.
Tool_80g_donation_ExampleStep_5: If 100% eligible, deduction and saving would double.
Tool_80g_donation_Step_2: If limited, base = min(donation, 10% of income).
Tool_80g_donation_Info_Purpose: Estimate 80G deduction and tax saving on charitable donations.
Tool_80g_donation_Info_WhyKnow: Wrong category or limit assumptions overstate the tax benefit.
Tool_80g_donation_Info_RealLife: Donations to approved trusts, PM CARES, and notified funds (old regime).
Tool_80g_donation_Info_Alt_Tax_When: For full-year tax after all deductions.
Tool_80g_donation_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra models 50%/100% eligibility and the optional 10% income cap with slab saving.
